bibliographic_data.title: Explainable Galaxy Interaction Prediction with Hybrid Attention Mechanisms
bibliographic_data.abstract: Galaxy interaction classification remains challenging due to complex morphological patterns and the limited interpretability of deep learning models. We propose an attentive neural ensemble that combines AG-XCaps, H-SNN, and ResNet-GRU architectures, trained on the Galaxy Zoo DESI dataset and enhanced with LIME to enable explainable predictions. The model achieves Precision = 0.95, Recall = 1.00, F1 = 0.97, and Accuracy = 96%, outperforming a Random Forest baseline by significantly reducing false positives (23 vs. 70). This lightweight (0.45 MB) and scalable framework provides an interpretable and efficient solution for large-scale surveys such as Euclid and LSST, advancing data-driven studies of galaxy evolution.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes an attentive neural ensemble combining AG-XCaps, H-SNN, and ResNet-GRU architectures to predict galaxy interactions with high accuracy while enabling explainable predictions.
core_content.key_methodology: The model uses a hybrid architecture that combines capsule networks (AG-XCaps), spiking neural networks (H-SNN), and recurrent neural networks (ResNet-GRU) trained on the Galaxy Zoo DESI dataset. It is enhanced with LIME to provide interpretability.
outcomes: Achieved high precision, recall, F1 score, and accuracy in galaxy interaction prediction.
Outperformed a Random Forest baseline by significantly reducing false positives.
Provided an interpretable solution for large-scale surveys like Euclid and LSST.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper addresses the challenge of predicting galaxy interactions using a hybrid attention mechanism approach. Galaxy interactions, such as mergers and tidal distortions, are complex processes that drive structural transformations in galaxies. The authors propose an attentive neural ensemble combining AG-XCaps, H-SNN, and ResNet-GRU architectures to predict these interactions accurately while maintaining interpretability. The model is trained on the Galaxy Zoo DESI dataset and enhanced with LIME for explainable predictions. Key outcomes include high precision (0.95), recall (1.00), F1 score (0.97), and accuracy (96%). This lightweight framework provides an efficient solution for large-scale surveys, outperforming a Random Forest baseline by significantly reducing false positives.
